threat_model.name: Acme E-Commerce Platform v3 — STRIDE + MITRE ATT&CK

threats.title: Credential stuffing attack on Cognito authentication
threats.description: Attacker uses leaked credential databases to attempt mass authentication against Cognito user pool. Without advanced rate limiting, thousands of accounts can be tested per minute.
mitigations: Enable Cognito advanced security (risk-based adaptive auth)
Enforce MFA for all user accounts
Implement CAPTCHA after 3 failed attempts
Monitor for anomalous login patterns via CloudWatch

threats.title: Payment amount tampering in transit
threats.description: Attacker intercepts or modifies payment request between Order Service and Payment Service to alter transaction amount. Even with TLS, application-layer integrity validation is critical for PCI compliance.
threats.mitre_technique: T1565.002 — Data Manipulation: Transmitted Data Manipulation
mitigations: HMAC signature on payment payloads
Server-side price validation from catalog DB
Mutual TLS between Order and Payment services
Idempotency keys to prevent replay

threats.title: Order transaction log manipulation
threats.description: Malicious insider or compromised service modifies order records in RDS without audit trail, enabling dispute of completed transactions.
threats.mitre_technique: T1070.003 — Indicator Removal: Clear Command History
mitigations: Enable RDS audit logging to CloudWatch
Immutable audit log in separate AWS account
Database row-level versioning
CloudTrail for API-level actions

threats.title: PII exfiltration via SQL injection in User Service
threats.description: SQL injection vulnerability in User Service allows attacker to extract full user table containing names, emails, addresses, and hashed passwords. Spring Data JPA typically prevents this, but custom native queries are vulnerable.
mitigations: Parameterized queries for ALL database access
WAF SQL injection rule set
Database activity monitoring (Guardduty RDS)
Encrypt PII columns with application-level encryption

threats.title: S3 bucket misconfiguration exposes customer uploads
threats.description: Overly permissive S3 bucket policy or ACL allows public read access to customer-uploaded documents (receipts, profile photos). Common misconfiguration in production.
mitigations: Enable S3 Block Public Access at account level
Use CloudFront signed URLs for media access
S3 bucket policy restricts to VPC endpoint only
AWS Config rule: s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited

threats.title: Application-layer DDoS via cart abandonment flood
threats.description: Attacker creates millions of shopping carts without checkout, exhausting Redis memory and backend connections. Standard volumetric DDoS protection doesn't catch this application-logic abuse.
threats.mitre_technique: T1498.001 — Network Denial of Service: Direct Network Flood
mitigations: Cart TTL in Redis (30 min inactive expiry)
Rate limit cart creation per IP/session
AWS Shield Advanced with response team
Auto-scaling with circuit breaker pattern

threats.title: Container escape via Fargate task role escalation
threats.description: Compromised container exploits overly-permissive ECS task role to access other AWS services (S3, RDS, Secrets Manager) beyond intended scope. Task roles often have broader permissions than needed.
mitigations: Minimal IAM policies per service (least privilege)
Separate task execution role from task role
VPC endpoints to restrict service access
GuardDuty for anomalous API call detection

threats.title: Supply chain attack via compromised container image
threats.description: Attacker pushes malicious image to ECR or compromises base image dependency. Without image signing and scanning, poisoned containers deploy to production.
threats.mitre_technique: T1195.002 — Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
mitigations: ECR image scanning on push
Image signing with AWS Signer
Immutable image tags
Base image pinning with digest verification

threats.title: Session hijacking via Redis cache extraction
threats.description: Redis cluster without AUTH or encryption allows network-adjacent attacker to dump all session tokens, enabling mass account takeover.
mitigations: Enable Redis AUTH with Secrets Manager rotation
Enable in-transit encryption (TLS)
Restrict Redis security group to application subnets only
Short session TTL (15 minutes)

threats.title: API key theft enables unauthorized API access
threats.description: Exposed API keys in client-side JavaScript or Git repositories allow attackers to call backend APIs directly, bypassing frontend security controls.
threats.mitre_technique: T1552.001 — Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
mitigations: Use short-lived JWT tokens instead of API keys
Cognito authorizer on API Gateway
API key rotation every 90 days
Secret scanning in CI/CD pipeline

threats.title: Lambda function injection via SQS message poisoning
threats.description: Attacker injects malicious payload into SQS queue that triggers code execution in Lambda function. Deserialization vulnerabilities in message processing can lead to RCE.
threats.mitre_technique: T1059.006 — Command and Scripting Interpreter: Python
mitigations: Input validation and schema enforcement on all SQS messages
Lambda execution role with minimal permissions
Dead letter queue for malformed messages
CloudWatch alarm on Lambda errors

threats.title: Secrets leakage via CloudWatch log exposure
threats.description: Application logs contain unmasked secrets (database passwords, API keys, Stripe tokens) that get shipped to CloudWatch. Anyone with log read access sees production credentials.
threats.mitre_technique: T1552.005 — Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
mitigations: Structured logging with automatic secret redaction
CloudWatch log group encryption with CMK
IAM policy restricts log read access
Automated secret detection in log streams

threats.title: Search query injection causing OpenSearch cluster overload
threats.description: Crafted search queries with deep aggregations or regex patterns consume all cluster resources, causing search functionality outage for all users.
threats.mitre_technique: T1499.003 — Endpoint Denial of Service: Application Exhaustion Flood
mitigations: Query complexity limits on OpenSearch
Search request timeout (5 seconds)
Circuit breaker on search service
Rate limit search requests per user

threats.title: ML model poisoning via fraudulent training data
threats.description: Attacker systematically submits transactions designed to skew fraud detection model, causing it to accept genuinely fraudulent transactions.
threats.mitre_technique: T1565.001 — Data Manipulation: Stored Data Manipulation
mitigations: Model retraining requires manual approval
Anomaly detection on training data distribution
A/B testing new models before production rollout
Human review for high-value transactions

threats.title: Stripe webhook spoofing with forged signatures
threats.description: Attacker sends fake Stripe webhook events to the payment service, potentially marking unpaid orders as paid or triggering refunds.
mitigations: Verify Stripe webhook signature on every event
Restrict webhook endpoint to Stripe IP ranges
Idempotent webhook processing
Reconcile webhook events with Stripe API

threats.title: GraphQL introspection exposes internal schema
threats.description: Catalog Service uses GraphQL with introspection enabled in production, exposing full data model, internal types, and mutation operations to attackers.
threats.mitre_technique: T1592.004 — Gather Victim Host Information: Client Configurations
mitigations: Disable GraphQL introspection in production
Implement field-level authorization
Query depth limiting
Allowlisted query patterns

threats.title: JWT token manipulation via weak signing algorithm
threats.description: Auth Service signs JWTs with HS256 symmetric key. If key is leaked or attacker uses algorithm confusion (none/RS256→HS256), they can forge tokens with admin privileges.
threats.mitre_technique: T1134.001 — Access Token Manipulation: Token Impersonation
mitigations: Use RS256 asymmetric signing with key rotation
Explicit algorithm validation on token verification
Short token expiry (15 minutes) with refresh tokens
Token binding to client fingerprint

threats.title: Event injection via compromised EventBridge rule
threats.description: Attacker who gains access to EventBridge can inject fabricated domain events (e.g., fake order completions, fraudulent inventory updates) that downstream services trust implicitly.
mitigations: Event schema validation on all consumers
IAM policies restrict PutEvents to specific services
Event source verification
Anomaly detection on event volume
